title: Construction and building trades n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate, usually through subordinate supervisory personnel, activities concerned with the construction and maintenance of structures, facilities, and systems. Participate in the conceptual development of a construction project and oversee its organization, scheduling, budgeting, and implementation. Includes managers in specialized construction fields, such as carpentry or plumbing. Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems. Develop plans for surface transportation projects, according to established engineering standards and state or federal construction policy. Prepare designs, specifications, or estimates for transportation facilities. Plan modifications of existing streets, highways, or freeways to improve traffic flow. (Based on 29 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Take appropriate safety precautions and monitor conditions before starting diving expeditions.
Install, inspect, clean, or repair piping or valves in various structures.
Inspect and test the condition of underwater structures such as docks, ships, or pipelines using various equipment.
Install pilings or footings for structures like piers or bridges, and provide support using sandbags.
Repair underwater structures below the water line, such as ships or bridge foundations, using various tools.
Read blueprints and specifications to determine job requirements and select appropriate materials based on their insulation properties.
Measure, cut, and install insulation for structures, including covering surfaces and preparing for installation.
Construct and install masonry units, including repairing cracks and cleaning excess mortar from surfaces.
Erect and repair barriers, fences, and gates, ensuring proper alignment and secure attachment.
Mix and pour concrete for various applications, including setting posts or structural repairs.
Remove hazardous materials such as asbestos or lead, ensuring adherence to safety procedures.
Waterproof or restore concrete surfaces using appropriate compounds, and install fixtures in freshly poured concrete for a decorative finish.
Signal truck driver to position truck for pouring concrete and monitor environmental conditions affecting curing throughout the process.
Spread, level, and smooth concrete using various tools, and mold expansion joints and edges with appropriate edging tools.
Inspect buildings for required weatherization measures and recommend carbon-reducing techniques according to energy regulations.
Prepare hazardous materials for storage or removal, build containment areas for safety, and manage waste disposal according to proper procedures.
Remove or limit contamination after hazardous substance emergencies and perform minor repairs using basic tools.
Prepare cost estimates for projects, develop construction budgets, and prepare or assist in the preparation of bids or contracts.
Plan, schedule, and coordinate construction activities, direct craft work, and ensure compliance with building codes and safety regulations.
Erect metal or precast concrete components for structures and verify the alignment of structural members during assembly.
Conduct pre-building assessments and implement environmental protection programs, ensuring responsible design and building activities.
Install module array interconnect wiring, implementing measures to disable arrays during installation, and install required labels on solar system components and hardware.
Program, adjust, or configure inverters and controls for desired set points and operating modes, and determine photovoltaic (PV) system designs based on customer needs and site conditions.
Select mechanical designs, installation equipment, or plans that conform to environmental, architectural, structural, site, and code requirements.
Measure and analyze system performance and operating parameters to assess condition of systems or equipment, and visually inspect and test photovoltaic (PV) modules or systems.
Activate photovoltaic (PV) systems to verify functionality and conformity to performance expectations, and perform routine maintenance on various PV system components.
Identify installation locations with proper orientation, area, solar access, or structural integrity for PV arrays, and diagram layouts for PV arrays and equipment.
Examine designs to determine current requirements for all parts of the PV system electrical circuit, and check electrical installations for proper wiring and grounding.
Determine sizes, ratings, and locations for system overcurrent devices and grounding equipment, and identify connection interfaces for additional subpanels or connections to utility services.
Identify environmental risks and develop risk management strategies for civil engineering projects, and ensure compliance with environmental standards in construction materials evaluation.
Design or engineer drainage, erosion, or sedimentation control systems for transportation projects, and evaluate the operation and maintenance of water or wastewater systems.
Inspect completed transportation projects to ensure compliance with environmental regulations and safety standards, and monitor construction sites for adherence to building codes.
Analyze survey reports, maps, and blueprints to ensure compliance with legal requirements and safety regulations, and direct or participate in surveying to lay out installations.
Evaluate construction project costs and estimate project feasibility based on material, equipment, and labor costs, and prepare project budgets and schedules.
Conduct inspections to monitor projects for structural quality and conformance to specifications and codes, and conduct environmental impact studies related to wastewater treatment.
Design transportation systems or structures with sustainable materials or products, and oversee the construction of decentralized or on-site wastewater treatment systems.
Supervise the maintenance or repair of transportation systems or components, and direct engineering activities to ensure compliance with safety and governmental regulations.
Educate customers on energy efficiency or answer questions regarding energy-efficient appliances and costs.
Collect and analyze field data related to energy usage and calculate potential energy savings.
Analyze energy bills, including utility rates, to gather historical energy usage data and determine building use patterns for energy needs.
Inspect and evaluate building envelopes and systems to assess energy consumption and perform tests to locate air leaks.
Measure energy usage with various devices and prepare audit reports containing analysis results and recommendations.
Plan and conduct field surveys for new sites, evaluate project details for environmental compliance, and monitor construction for regulatory adherence.
Inspect project sites and evaluate contractor work to ensure design conformity and compliance with codes.
Prepare reports documenting project activities, including blueprints and specifications to determine material requirements.
Analyze proposed site factors, draft dimensional drawings, and report maintenance problems to supervisors for resolution.
Conduct materials testing and analysis using engineering knowledge and tools to ensure quality and compliance.
Inspect and test fire protection and detection systems to verify compliance with standards and procedures.
